mainEntity.name: What is the time limit for income tax appeal?

acceptedAnswer.text: CIT(A): 30 days from service of order. ITAT: 60 days from CIT(A) order. One day's delay requires condonation petition — discretionary, not automatic. File at least 2-3 days before the deadline to account for portal glitches.
CIT(A): ₹250 (assessed income up to ₹2L), ₹500 (₹2L-₹5L), ₹1,000 (above ₹5L). ITAT: ₹500-₹1,500 depending on income. Negligible compared to tax at stake.

National Faceless Appeal Centre processes all CIT(A) appeals electronically. No physical hearing (in most cases). Written submissions and documents are paramount — the quality of the paper record determines the outcome.

mainEntity.name: Can you get a stay of demand during appeal?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Under Section 220(6), the AO can stay demand collection pending appeal. We file stay applications with grounds and case law. If the AO refuses, we escalate to the CIT under Section 220(7) or approach the ITAT for stay.

CIT can revise an AO order if erroneous and prejudicial to revenue — even if no appeal was filed. Effectively, the department reopens your case through a different route. We defend against Section 263 at the show cause stage and, if revision is passed, appeal to ITAT.
